mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate above 3.5% signals restrictive monetary policy that raises discount rates on equity valuations and increases debt service costs for corporations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 3.5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window during tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate below 3.8% indicates tight labor market with wage pressure and reduced slack, signaling elevated inflation persistence that constrains Federal Reserve easing and sustains equity headwinds over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of triggered instances, or correlation between UNRATE and subsequent 60-day returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05) over the full sample.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) decline more than 2% month-over-month signals weakening consumer demand and reduced earnings growth expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 58% of instances following RSAFS declines > 2%, or Rank IC between RSAFS momentum and forward returns is below 0.025 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth above 1% year-over-year combined with FEDFUNDS above 3.5% indicates supply-side constraints meeting restrictive monetary policy, creating stagflationary pressure that depresses equity valuations over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 104.52 and FEDFUNDS at 3.78% as of 2022-11-01
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of co-triggered instances, or the interaction term (INDPRO × FEDFUNDS) shows Rank IC < 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) decline below 1400 thousand units annualized signals deteriorating residential investment and forward-looking demand weakness, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as market reprices growth expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1425 thousand units as of 2022-10-01
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances when HOUST < 1400, or Rank IC between HOUST and 5-day forward returns is below 0.015 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates below 100 thousand persons month-over-month while UNRATE remains below 4% signals labor market softening without Fed accommodation, creating uncertainty about earnings sustainability and producing negative 20-day forward returns.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly growth < 100 thousand AND UNRATE < 4%
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 153548 thousand and UNRATE at 3.7% as of 2022-11-01
regime_hint: labor market deceleration with tight conditions
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 58% of co-triggered instances, or the interaction effect (PAYEMS growth × UNRATE) shows Rank IC < 0.025 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) year-over-year growth above 8% combined with FEDFUNDS below 4% indicates real rates remain negative, signaling monetary policy lag that sustains inflation expectations and equity volatility, producing negative 60-day forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 298.35 and FEDFUNDS at 3.78% as of 2022-11-01
regime_hint: high inflation with monetary policy lag
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of co-triggered instances, or the interaction term (inflation gap × FEDFUNDS) shows Rank IC < 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 1% annualized while RSAFS contracts month-over-month signals recession entry with demand collapse, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as earnings forecasts are revised downward.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth < 1% annualized AND RSAFS month-over-month decline
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 20039.41 billion and RSAFS at 689443 million as of 2022-11-01
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of co-triggered instances, or the interaction term (GDP growth × RSAFS momentum) shows Rank IC < 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window.
